name: Exponential Function Class and Derivative Inclusion
statement_text:
For any real θ and positive integer ξ, E (θ) ∈ C(ξ) and
E (θ) ∈ D(ξ).
proof_text:
Proof. To show that E (θ) ∈ C(ξ), we note that E (θ) (t) = γ(t)−1 θ r(t) and that
if E (θ) is substituted for α in (388c), then both sides are equal to
                              θ r(t)+r(t1 )+···+r(tm )+1
                                                                          .
              (r(t) + r(t1 ) + · · · + r(tm ) + 1)γ(t)γ(t1 ) · · · γ(tm )
To prove that E (θ) ∈ D(ξ), substitute E into (388d). We find
                  r(t)                  r(u)            1    1
                              +                      =     ·     .
proof_latex:
\begin{proof}
To show that $E^{(\theta)} \in C^{(\xi)}$, we note that $E^{(\theta)}(t) = \gamma(t)^{-1} \theta^{r(t)}$ and that
if $E^{(\theta)}$ is substituted for $\alpha$ in \eqref{eq:388c}, then both sides are equal to
\[
\frac{\theta^{r(t)+r(t_1)+\cdots+r(t_m)+1}}{(r(t) + r(t_1) + \cdots + r(t_m) + 1)\gamma(t)\gamma(t_1) \cdots \gamma(t_m)}.
\]
To prove that $E^{(\theta)} \in D^{(\xi)}$, substitute $E$ into \eqref{eq:388d}. We find
\[
\frac{r(t)}{(r(t) + r(u))\gamma(t)\gamma(u)} + \frac{r(u)}{(r(t) + r(u))\gamma(t)\gamma(u)} = \frac{1}{\gamma(t)} \cdot \frac{1}{\gamma(u)}. \qedhere
\]
\end{proof}

variables.name: E
variables.definition: E is the elementary weight function (or the exact flow map) associated with the exact solution of an ODE, often used in the context of B-series or rooted trees.

variables.name: E^{(θ)}
variables.definition: E^{(θ)} is a specific member of the group G1 (or a related structure) parameterized by a real θ, representing a numerical method or transformation related to the exact solution.

variables.name: C(ξ)
variables.definition: C(ξ) is a subset of G1 defined by conditions on rooted trees up to order ξ, specifically satisfying equations (388c) and a relation involving α(t) and γ(t).

variables.name: D(ξ)
variables.definition: D(ξ) is a subset of G1 defined by a condition (388d) on rooted trees t and u where the order of t is at most ξ.

variables.name: G1
variables.definition: G1 is a group (or algebraic structure) whose members are functions on rooted trees, used to represent numerical methods for ODEs via B-series.

variables.name: ξ
variables.definition: ξ is a positive integer representing an order or bound on the size of trees considered in the conditions C(ξ) and D(ξ).

variables.name: θ
variables.definition: θ is a real parameter indexing the family E^{(θ)}.

preamble: The context involves algebraic structures (like G1) based on rooted trees to analyze numerical methods for ODEs. C(ξ) and D(ξ) are normal subgroups of G1 defined by specific conditions on tree functions, with E^{(θ)} being a particular element of interest.
